description:
After adding the Apple-only fields to fuse_operations and porting fuse_getattr/fill_* helpers to fill struct fuse_darwin_attr (the macFUSE-private replacement for libc::stat, see /usr/local/include/fuse3/fuse_common.h around line 897), mounts no longer crash and reach the correct callbacks. But `ls` of the mount root fails with EACCES because the kernel-visible attrs we wrote are mostly zero.

Concretely (with our daemon writing into fuse_darwin_attr at offset 0):

  We wrote:                      Kernel sees:
    ino   = 1                       Inode = 2          (kernel-synthesized)
    mode  = S_IFDIR | 0o755         (0000/d---------)  (mode bits stripped)
    nlink = 2                       Links = 0
    uid   = 502                     Uid: 502           CORRECT
    gid   = 20                      Gid: 20            CORRECT
    size  = 4096                    Size: 0
    {a,m,c}timespec = now           ~1 second after epoch

Layout of our fuse_darwin_attr is verified (test_fuse_darwin_attr_layout):
ino@0/u64, mode@8/u16, nlink@10/u16, uid@12/u32, gid@16/u32, rdev@20/i32,
atimespec@24, mtimespec@40, ctimespec@56, btimespec@72, bkuptimespec@88,
size@104/i64, blocks@112/i64, blksize@120/i32, flags@124/u32,
reserved[8]@128 — total 192 bytes. The fields that landed correctly
(uid@12, gid@16) confirm the layout is right.

So macFUSE's FSKit shim seems to honor only uid/gid from the getattr
return value and synthesize the rest from FSKit-side state, regardless of
what we write. That diverges from libfuse's documented contract on Linux
and on macFUSE-via-kext. Verifying this requires either:

  (a) Source access to macFUSE 5.2 to see how its FSKit dispatcher reads
      our fuse_operations callbacks (the github.com/macfuse/macfuse repo
      may have a 5.2 branch).
  (b) Reverse-engineering MFMount.framework / libfuse3.4.dylib to find
      where it constructs the FSKit FSItem attrs.
  (c) Filing an upstream issue.

Until that's sorted, FSKit on Tahoe yields a non-functional mount even
with all the structural fixes in place. The committed work in this branch
(libfuse 3.18 ABI port, Apple-only fuse_operations fields, fuse_darwin_attr
mirror, getattr port) is correct and useful regardless — it fixes latent
ABI bugs that would silently bite us on the kext path too. But realizing
working FSKit needs upstream cooperation or non-trivial RE.

Recommendation: keep the committed bindings work (it's right per the
documented headers), revert the runtime configuration to NOT default to
backend=fskit (back to the kext path on macFUSE, which on Apple Silicon
Tahoe needs Reduced Security mode anyway), and either (a) accept FUSE-T
NFS as the supported macOS path for now, or (b) raise the Darwin-attrs
question with macFUSE upstream.
title: macFUSE FSKit attr propagation: only uid/gid honored
